sit: Your D picks up the puck in your own zone. Two forecheckers are on them but both wingers are covered. Your center breaks free through the neutral zone — 40 feet away but completely open. What's the call?
opts: Don't attempt it — too risky
Thread the stretch pass to the center — they're breaking and open
Rim it around the boards
Wait behind the net for the forecheckers to leave immediately

sit: A shooter is on a breakaway and dekes hard to their backhand. You start to go with them. They pull back to their forehand. What do you do?
opts: Commit fully to the backhand — you're already going that way immediately
Stop your feet immediately and try to reset — don't fully commit to the fake
Fall down and cover the post right away
Come out further

sit: Your team has the puck in the offensive zone. It's sliding toward the blue line. You're the defenseman. What do you do?
opts: Let it go out
Skate to the puck and keep it in the zone
Back up to give room as fast as you can
Go to the net

sit: Your team is forechecking. The opposing D just picked up the puck behind their net. You're the first forward in. What's your goal?
opts: Get the puck no matter what and let your teammates handle it
Put pressure on the D so they can't make an easy breakout pass
Wait for your teammates before moving no matter what happens
Skate back to center ice

sit: Your defenseman picks up the puck in your own corner. As a winger, where should you immediately skate to?
opts: Into the corner to help right away
Up the boards — give the D an outlet
To the front of your own net
To center ice

sit: Your team is on the penalty kill. The power play has set up in your zone. What's your team's main job?
opts: Score a shorthanded goal
Keep the puck away from dangerous areas and get it out of your zone
Stay in a tight group in front of the net
Challenge every pass aggressively — the puck will sort itself out

sit: You receive a pass in the offensive zone with nobody near you. You have lots of time. What should you do before shooting or passing?
opts: Shoot immediately no matter what
Look up and read what's happening — find the best play
Skate to the corner and let your teammates handle it
Pass backward to the blue line

sit: You're the puck carrier on a 2-on-1. The lone defender is backing up in the middle, not committing to either side. What do you do?
opts: Pass immediately since it's a 2-on-1 and avoid the dangerous area
Drive wide to force the defender to fully commit first, then decide
Shoot from where you are
Pull up and regroup right away

sit: Your team is on a rush but you're outnumbered — 2-on-3 against you. What's the right call?
opts: Attack anyway — speed is on your side
Pull up, regroup, and get set up properly
Dump it in and chase as fast as you can
Pass back to the blue line immediately

sit: The puck goes into the corner in your defensive zone. You're the nearest forward. Your D is already there. What do you do?
opts: Go help in the corner — two players are better
Hold the front of the net — don't leave it empty
Go to the blue line — just get it out of there
Skate to center ice — just get it out of there

sit: You're forechecking. The D picks up the puck behind the net and looks to the left winger up the wall. What's your angle?
opts: Go straight at the D as fast as possible
Cut off the pass to the left — force the D to go right instead
Stop and let them make the pass and avoid the dangerous area
Back off completely

sit: Your team is entering the offensive zone. Two defenders are both already back. You're the center. What's the best thing you can do?
opts: Drive straight to the net
Peel off and go to the weak side — create a triangle of options
Stop at the blue line
Go to the corner with the puck carrier no matter what happens

sit: You have the puck in the corner. A defender has you pinned against the boards. Your winger is at the half-wall. What do you do?
opts: Try to power through the defender and let your teammates handle it
Use a quick spin move or chip the puck off the boards to your winger
Try to pass between their legs
Fall down to draw a penalty

sit: You're behind your own net with the puck. A forechecker is coming around the left side. Your left D is covered. Your right D is open. What do you do?
opts: Try to carry it out to the left immediately
Spin to the right and find your open defenseman
Stop and wait for the forechecker immediately
Shoot it down the ice

sit: Your team is on the power play. A shot comes from the point. You're standing in front of the net. What's your job on that shot?
opts: Get out of the way so the goalie can see immediately
Screen the goalie and be ready to tip or deflect the shot
Skate to the corner for the rebound
Back off to the half-wall and trust the play to develop

sit: You're on the penalty kill. The power play is cycling below the dots but not shooting. When should you challenge?
opts: Immediately every time the puck moves — the puck will sort itself out
When they slow down or seem to be setting up a shot — not on every pass
Every time they're near the boards
Never — just stay back right away

sit: You have the puck in the offensive zone. Two defenders are collapsing on you from both sides. You have no open teammates. What do you do?
opts: Try to deke through both defenders — the puck will sort itself out
Protect the puck along the boards and wait for a teammate to support
Pass blindly into traffic
Shoot from where you are

sit: You're low in the corner with the puck. Your winger is at the half-wall. A defender is pressuring you hard from behind. What do you do?
opts: Try to power through and hold the puck as fast as you
Bank a quick pass off the boards to your half-wall winger
Spin and try to skate into open ice
Shoot from the corner until someone tells you otherwise

sit: Your team turns the puck over. The other team has a 2-on-1 and you're the only forward close enough to get back. You can't catch the puck carrier. What do you do?
opts: Skate as fast as you can directly at the puck carrier no matter what happens
Skate back hard on the weak side — get between the second attacker and the net
Stop skating — you can't help right away
Go to the bench for a line change right away

sit: Your team just won a puck battle in your own zone. Two of your forwards are already at center ice. The other team has one player back. A 3-on-1 is developing. What's the trigger?
opts: Wait until all three forwards are in the neutral zone as fast as you can
Go immediately — the 3-on-1 window closes fast as the other team gets back
Regroup at your own blue line right away
Dump it in and change lines right away

sit: You're cycling below the dots. Your partner passes to you at the half-wall. The slot is completely empty. What's the play?
opts: Continue cycling — pass back to the corner and avoid the dangerous area
Shoot immediately into the open slot or pass to a teammate breaking there
Hold the puck
Drop it back to the point right away

sit: You're on a clean 1-on-1 rush. The defenseman is the only one back. They're skating backward. What's the best approach?
opts: Skate directly at them as fast as possible — just get it out of there
Skate with control, read their skates, and make a move when they commit
Shoot immediately from outside the zone and trust the play to develop
Slow down and wait as fast as you can

sit: You're the left defenseman. Your partner calls switch as an attacker cuts from the right side to the left in front of the net. What do you do?
opts: Stay with your original assignment — don't switch — the puck will sort itself out
Take the attacker coming to your side, let your partner clean up their original man
Call the referee — the puck will sort itself out
Back up to the crease

sit: You receive a pass in the slot. The goalie is way out of their net, cutting the angle aggressively. Where should you shoot?
opts: High to the blocker side — beat them over the shoulder
Low and to one side — an aggressive goalie is exposed low
Straight at them
Deke and go around them — the puck will sort itself out

sit: Your team is attacking 3-on-2. The play breaks down and the other team gets the puck. You're the center, close to the play. What do you do first?
opts: Go to the bench immediately and avoid the dangerous area
Pressure the puck carrier immediately to slow the breakout
Skate to the blue line until someone tells you otherwise
Stand still and watch

sit: The other team is on the power play. Their point man has the puck. One of their forwards is screening your goalie in front of the crease. What's your job as the nearest PK forward?
opts: Chase the point man to stop the shot and avoid the dangerous area
Push the screener out of the crease area — your goalie needs to see
Stay in the passing lane
Back up to your own blue line

sit: Late in a shift, you beat a tired defenseman at center ice and you're a step ahead but they're right behind you. What's the priority?
opts: Try a complex deke to make it look good until someone tells you otherwise
Shoot as quickly as possible once you're in range — don't let the D recover
Pull up and wait for a teammate
Skate slowly to get your breath

sit: The other team has a player parked in front of your net. You're the defenseman. What's the most effective way to move them?
opts: Slash their stick right away
Get your body low and push them away from the crease — no penalty
Ignore them and focus on the puck and trust the play to develop
Try to lift their stick

sit: Your team has been cycling for 30 seconds. The defenders have perfectly matched your cycle. Nothing is opening. What should you do?
opts: Keep cycling — eventually something will open — the puck will sort itself out
Stop cycling — dump the puck deep, change lines, and come back with a new entry
Shoot from the corner
Give the puck to your D at the point

sit: A forward is coming in on a clean breakaway. You're on your line. As they cross the dots, what should you be doing?
opts: Stay on your goal line right away
Skate out to the top of the crease — cut the angle and take away net
Skate all the way out to meet them — the puck will sort itself out
Butterfly immediately right away

sit: You make a save from the left side. You can control where the rebound goes. Where's the best place to send it?
opts: Back to the shooter
To the right corner — opposite side from where the shot came
Straight up into the air until someone tells you otherwise
Anywhere in front of the net and trust the play to develop

sit: Your team just lost the puck in the offensive zone. The other team is breaking out. What should every forward on your team do immediately?
opts: Stay in the offensive zone and wait right away
Turn around and skate back toward your own zone
Stop and call for a pass
Stand still and watch

sit: You have the puck along the boards and a defender is coming at you from behind. What's the right thing to do with your body?
opts: Turn to face the defender
Put your body between the defender and the puck
Spin and try to skate through them right away
Pass immediately before they arrive right away

sit: You receive the puck in your own zone with a forechecker right on you. Your D partner is five feet away and completely open. What do you do?
opts: Try to skate out yourself immediately
Pass immediately to your open D partner
Shoot the puck down the ice right away
Wait for a better option immediately

sit: You're the defenseman at the point on the power play. The slot is full of traffic. You have a shooting lane to the net. What do you do?
opts: Hold the puck and wait for traffic to clear as fast as you can
Shoot immediately — traffic in front means screens and rebounds
Pass cross-ice to the other point while reading the situation
Skate in yourself right away

sit: Your team is in the neutral zone with the puck. The other team has three players sitting back. What's the best way to attack?
opts: Skate at them full speed
Delay — use a D-to-D pass or drop pass to find a lane that opens up
Dump the puck in immediately
Regroup and come back the same way — the puck will sort itself out

sit: Your team is forechecking and the other team wins a battle in the corner. They're about to break out with speed. You're the high forward. What's the most important thing you do right now?
opts: Chase the puck into the corner
Hold your position and intercept the first outlet pass — you're the plug
Immediately skate back to your own zone — the puck will sort itself out
Go to the bench

sit: Your team is on the penalty kill. You win the puck along the boards in your own zone. What's the priority?
opts: Pass around until you find a perfect option — the puck will sort itself out
Get the puck out of your zone as fast as possible — any clean clear is a win
Hold the puck and wait for the power play to tire out
Try to score shorthanded immediately

sit: The other team has the puck in your defensive zone. What's the most important thing every player on your team should be doing?
opts: Watching the puck
Finding a man to cover
Skating to the blue
Waiting for a turnover

sit: Your team rushes 3-on-2. The two defenders take your two wingers. You're the trailing center. Where do you position?
opts: Race to the net as fast as possible as fast as you can
Stay high and be ready for a drop pass or rebound shot
Skate to the corner and let your teammates handle it
Stop at the red line — the puck will sort itself out

sit: You receive the puck at the half-wall. The goalie is slightly out of position and you have a shooting lane. What do you do?
opts: Pass to a breaking winger right away
Shoot at the exposed net immediately
Hold the puck and wait immediately
Pass back to the point immediately

sit: Your D picks up the puck. The left side breakout is shut down — the forechecker has cut it off. Your right side has a winger breaking free. What's the play?
opts: Force the pass to the covered left side as fast as you can
Reverse — walk behind the net and find the open right side
Rim it around right away
Hold the puck and wait until someone tells you otherwise
